title: Durvalumab and Tremelimumab in Combination With First-Line Chemotherapy in Advanced Solid Tumors
conditions: Small Cell Lung Carcinoma, Carcinoma, Squamous Cell of Head and Neck, Stomach Neoplasms, Triple Negative Breast Neoplasms, Ovarian Neoplasms, Fallopian Tube Neoplasms, Peritoneal Neoplasms, Esophagogastric Junction Neoplasms, Carcinoma, Pancreatic Ductal, Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
interventions: paclitaxel + carboplatin (DRUG), carboplatin + etoposide (DRUG), gemcitabine + carboplatin (DRUG), nab-paclitaxel (paclitaxel-albumin) + carboplatin (DRUG), oxaliplatin + 5-fluorouracil (5FU) + leucovorin (calcium folinate/folinic acid) (DRUG), durvalumab (BIOLOGICAL), tremelimumab (BIOLOGICAL), nab-paclitaxel (paclitaxel-albumin) + gemcitabine (DRUG), cisplatin + 5-fluorouracil (5FU) (DRUG)
brief_summary: Durvalumab and Tremelimumab in combination with first-line chemotherapy in the following indications: Ovarian/peritoneal/fallopian tube cancer, SCCHN, TNBC, SCLC and gastric/GEJ cancer, PDAC, ESCC.
ai_summary: This completed Phase I/II trial (2016-2019) evaluated the combination of two immunotherapies (durvalumab and tremelimumab) with standard first-line chemotherapy regimens across multiple advanced solid tumors, including small cell lung cancer, head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, triple-negative breast cancer, ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal cancers, gastric/esophagogastric junction cancers, pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, and esophageal squamous cell carcinoma. The study tested various chemotherapy backbones (including platinum-based doublets and other standard regimens) combined with the immunotherapy agents

title: A Phase 1b/2 Study of T-DXd Combinations in HER2-positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
interventions: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (DRUG), Durvalumab (DRUG), Paclitaxel (DRUG), Pertuzumab (DRUG), Tucatinib (DRUG)
brief_summary: DESTINY-Breast07 will investigate the safety, tolerability, and anti-tumour activity of trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in combination with other anti-cancer agents in patients with HER2-positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
ai_summary: This Phase 1b/2 trial evaluates trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in combination with various anti-cancer agents (durvalumab, paclitaxel, pertuzumab, or tucatinib) for treating HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer. The study, sponsored by AstraZeneca, is currently active but no longer recruiting participants (started December 2020, expected completion January 2030). The trial aims to assess the safety, tolerability, and anti-tumor activity of these T-DXd combination therapies, with outcomes data pending.

title: A Study of Dato-DXd With or Without Durvalumab Versus Investigator's Choice of Therapy in Patients With Stage I-III Triple-negative Breast Cancer Without Pathological Complete Response Following Neoadjuvant Therapy (TROPION-Breast03)
brief_summary: This is a Phase III, randomized, open-label, 3-arm, multicenter, international study assessing the efficacy and safety of Dato-DXd with or without durvalumab compared with ICT in participants with stage I to III TNBC with residual invasive disease in the breast and/or axillary lymph nodes at surgical resection following neoadjuvant systemic therapy.
ai_summary: This Phase III trial (TROPION-Breast03) evaluates treatment options for patients with stage I-III triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) who have residual disease after neoadjuvant therapy and surgery. The study compares Dato-DXd (an antibody-drug conjugate) alone or combined with durvalumab (immunotherapy) against investigator's choice of therapy (capecitabine or pembrolizumab). The trial is currently active but not recruiting new patients, with completion expected in January 2030; no outcome data are yet available.

title: First Time in Human Study of AZD8701 With or Without Durvalumab in Participants With Advanced Solid Tumours
conditions: Clear Cell Renal Cell Cancer, Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer, Triple Negative Breast Neoplasms, Squamous Cell Cancer of Head and Neck, Small Cell Lung Cancer, Gastroesophageal Cancer, Melanoma, Cervical Cancer, Advanced Solid Tumours
brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, Immunogenicity and Antitumor Activity of AZD8701 Alone and in Combination with Durvalumab (MEDI4736) in Adult Subjects with Select Advanced Solid Tumors
ai_summary: This completed Phase I trial (2020-2024) evaluated AZD8701, an investigational drug, alone and combined with durvalumab (an immunotherapy) in patients with various advanced solid tumors including clear cell renal cell cancer, non-small-cell lung cancer, triple negative breast cancer, head and neck squamous cell cancer, and several other malignancies. The study assessed safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, immunogenicity, and antitumor activity of this novel combination approach, though specific outcome results are not provided in this summary.

title: A Phase I/II Study of MEDI4736 in Combination With Olaparib in Patients With Advanced Solid Tumors.
brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to look at the effectiveness, safety, and antitumor activity of study drugs MEDI4736 in combination with olaparib (modules 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 7) and MEDI4736 in combination with olaparib and bevacizumab (module 6). It will also examine what happens to the study drugs in the body and investigate how well the combination between MEDI4736, olaparib and bevacizumab is tolerated.
ai_summary: This Phase I/II trial evaluates MEDI4736 (durvalumab, an immunotherapy) combined with olaparib (a PARP inhibitor), with or without bevacizumab, in patients with advanced solid tumors including ovarian, breast, small cell lung, and gastric cancers. The study, sponsored by AstraZeneca, is currently active but not recruiting new participants (started March 2016, expected completion September 2026). The trial aims to assess the safety, tolerability, effectiveness, and antitumor activity of these drug combinations, as well as their pharmacokinetic properties.

title: Study of Neoadjuvant Olaparib Monotherapy and Olaparib and Durvalumab Combination in HER2 Negative BRCAm Breast Cancer
interventions: Neoadjuvant Olaparib monotherapy group (DRUG), Neoadjuvant combination therapy with olaparib plus durvalumab (COMBINATION_PRODUCT)
brief_summary:
This study to learn more about olaparib and olaparib plus durvalumab combination therapy and also to better understand the studied disease, breast cancer, and associated health problems.

Olaparib is a type of drug called a PARP (poly \[adenosine diphosphate-ribose\] polymerase) inhibitor. PARP inhibitors can destroy cancer cells that are not good at repairing DNA damage. Olaparib is also approved by US Food and Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA) and in other countries for treating women with BRCA-mutated, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-negative metastatic breast cancer.

Durvalumab is a type of anticancer drug called immunotherapy that targets cancer cells by blocking the signal that prevents the immune system from seeing the cancer cell. Your immune system can then attack and kill the cancer cells. Durvalumab is approved by the FDA and the EMA for the treatment of patients with locally advanced non-small cell lung cancer after receiving chemoradiation therapy and extensive-stage small cell lung cancer in combination with chemotherapy.

Some parts of this study are experimental, which means that durvalumab and the combination of olaparib and durvalumab are still in the development stage for the treatment of breast cancer, and they are not approved for treatment of breast cancer, except for use in research studies like this.
ai_summary: This trial evaluates neoadjuvant (pre-surgery) treatment for HER2-negative, BRCA-mutated breast cancer, testing olaparib monotherapy (a PARP inhibitor) versus olaparib combined with durvalumab (an immunotherapy drug). The study is currently active but not recruiting new participants, having started in November 2022 with an expected completion date of December 2026. While olaparib is FDA-approved for metastatic BRCA-mutated breast cancer, both durvalumab and the combination therapy remain experimental for this breast cancer population, with outcomes still pending.

title: A Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability and Anti-tumour Activity of Ascending Doses of Selumetinib in Combination With MEDI4736 and Selumetinib in Combination With MEDI4736 and Tremelimumab in Patients With Advanced Solid Tumours
conditions: Lung Cancer, Melanoma, Head and Neck Carcinoma, Gastroesophageal Cancer, Breast Cancer, Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma, Colorectal Cancer, Biliary Tract Cancer
brief_summary: This is a Phase I, open-label, multi-centre, drug combination study of double and triple combination oral selumetinib (AZD6244 Hyd-sulfate) plus intravenous (IV) MEDI4736 and oral selumetinib plus IV MEDI4736 and IV tremelimumab in patients with advanced solid tumours.
ai_summary: This completed Phase I trial (2015-2019) evaluated the safety, tolerability, and anti-tumor activity of selumetinib combined with MEDI4736 (durvalumab), with or without tremelimumab, in patients with various advanced solid tumors including lung cancer, melanoma, colorectal cancer, and pancreatic adenocarcinoma. The open-label, multi-center study tested escalating doses of these immunotherapy and MEK inhibitor combinations administered orally and intravenously. The trial was sponsored by AstraZeneca and has been completed, though specific outcome results are not provided in this summary.
